question_text: Let Y1 < · · · < Yn be order statistics from a continuous distribution symmetric about its mean µ. Find the smallest n (in integer) such that P(Y1 < µ < Yn) ≥0.99.
solution:
By symmetry and continuity, P(X ≤µ) = P(X ≥µ) = 1
2 and ties have probability 0. Thus
P(Y1 < µ < Yn) = 1 −P(all ≤µ) −P(all ≥µ) = 1 −2 �1
n = 1 −21−n.
Require 1 −21−n ≥0.99 ⇒21−n ≤0.01 ⇒2n−1 ≥100. Hence n −1 ≥log2 100 ≈6.6438 so
the smallest integer is 8 .
Quick Tip
For symmetric continuous distributions, probabilities involving µ often reduce to pow-
ers of 1
2 since observations fall on either side independently with probability 1
2 each.
